
#
# --- T3 compile-only fixtures (wasm32-wasi-p2 compile correctness) ---
# Benchmark suite (in benchmarks/ directory, not test fixtures)
# Categories: scalars, strings, vecs, structs, closures, modules, traits,
# Component Model compile-only tests
# Component Model type-error tests (canonical ABI not yet implemented)
# Every .ark entry point must be listed here.
# Fixture manifest — single source of truth for test fixtures.
# Format: kind:path (relative to tests/fixtures/)
# Issue #568 — selfhost analysis API surface (text → AST → symbols → diagnostics)
# Issue #588 — language surface uplift fixtures (selfhost compiler coverage)
# Issue #601 — TypeScheme / let-generalization
# Issue #611 — T3 dead function elimination safety with exported roots
# Kinds: run, diag, fmt, module-run, module-diag, t3-compile, component-compile, component-wit-parse, component-world-wit, component-world-error, compile-error
# Optimisation tests
# Parser-acceptance fixtures (slice 1 of issue #039): not compiled/run yet — resolver pending
# SIMD fixtures (issue #698)
# The 100,000-depth countdown proves TCO prevents stack overflow.
# The harness reads this file instead of globbing.
# These verify that the T3 WasmGC emitter compiles without errors.
# countdown_let/sum_let use explicit `let result = f(…); result` — the MIR
# generics, control flow, operators, match, for loops, ? operator.
# issue 449 / MIR-01: regression fixtures — T1 (run) coverage alongside t3-compile entries above
# issue 487: Vec<Struct> len() correctness (fix in mir_lower)
# opportunistic: proves return_call emission via the let-call-return pattern (#090).
# std::host shared capabilities T1/T3 smoke matrix (#138)
# tail-call pass does NOT rewrite these; the T3 emitter's opportunistic
# tail_call: uses T3/WasmGC emitter (wasm32-wasi-p2) which emits return_call for tail recursion.
# tail_call_ref: proves return_call_ref emission for indirect tail calls
# try_emit_let_call_tail_return detects and emits return_call instead.
# via typed GC function references (FnRef local in tail position, #492).
